behavior_summary: Handles the player 'Look/Examine' interaction for the TOSSROCK object.
When examined it displays a single short bark text: "rock " (appears to be a minimal or placeholder description).
Immediately suspends/ends the U8P_TOSSROCK::look process after showing the text; there is no branching, menu, or state modification.
notable_control_flow: Entry into the U8P_TOSSROCK::look process triggers a single output (Bark) then a suspend/terminate node — linear control flow with no conditions.
No calls to other game systems, no reads or writes of global flags, and no local state changes are present.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text "rock " an artifact of decompilation/raw text or intentionally present in-game?
Does the 'suspend' node end the U8P_TOSSROCK::look process completely or yield to other engine processes in some contexts (inferred to terminate/suspend the U8P_TOSSROCK::look handler)?
Was this minimal description intended as a placeholder for a longer description or is the object intentionally terse?

main_function.name: U8P_TOSSROCK::look
main_function.purpose: Provide a short description when the player examines the TOSSROCK object and then end/suspend the U8P_TOSSROCK::look process.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine when the player uses the Look/Examine verb on this object (processType 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_TOSSROCK::look
function_map.role: Respond to U8P_TOSSROCK::look/examine events for the object by emitting a short descriptive bark and suspending the process.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Handles the player 'Look/Examine' interaction for the TOSSROCK object.
